data.title: Hello, world from Hugo
data.excerpt: A blog post stored as a JSON file in @webhouse/cms, synced to Hugo markdown via a 100-line Go script.
data.content:
## The setup

Hugo expects markdown files in `content/posts/`. **scripts/sync-content.go** reads the JSON files in `_webhouse-content/posts/`, extracts the fields, and writes them as Hugo-compatible markdown with TOML front matter.

Run the sync, then `hugo build` or `hugo serve`. The CMS admin writes the JSON, the sync script regenerates the markdown.

## Why this approach

Hugo's content model is opinionated — front matter + markdown. Our JSON model is more structured (separate fields for excerpt, tags, author). The 100-line sync script bridges the two without losing fidelity.

For production, run `go run scripts/sync-content.go` as a pre-build step in your CI/CD or content webhook.
data.author: Christian Broberg
